command_stdout: [plan] collect timeline, auth, powershell, registry, network evidence
[correct] CLAIM-004 revoked; rerouted into powershell + network evidence

scenes.label: Recorded demo
scenes.title: Prove 17 DFIR claims in a live run.
scenes.narration: TraceWarden SIFT proves seventeen DFIR claims in a recorded run. The command writes receipts before findings reach the report.

scenes.label: Terminal
scenes.title: One command starts the case.
scenes.proof: PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m tracewarden run examples/evidence/case-alpha --out runs/demo-video
scenes.narration: The demo starts in the repo root. One command runs the packaged case-alpha evidence and writes a fresh output folder.

scenes.label: Stdout
scenes.title: The recorded run evaluates 17 claims.
scenes.proof: Actual stdout captured from the CLI.
scenes.narration: The agent seals the evidence root, calls read-only parsers, evaluates seventeen claims, and writes the reports. This is the stdout from the run.

scenes.label: Self-correction
scenes.title: CLAIM-004 gets revoked.
scenes.narration: CLAIM zero zero four fails. The timeline shows a signed updater path, and no second source supports malicious activity, so the verifier revokes it.

scenes.label: Reroute
scenes.title: TraceWarden follows stronger evidence.
scenes.proof: PowerShell and network parsers support the replacement claim.
scenes.narration: TraceWarden reroutes into PowerShell and network evidence. CLAIM zero zero nine survives because two parsers point to the same attacker script.

scenes.label: Receipt
scenes.title: Judges can inspect every field.
scenes.proof: File, line, parser, hash, tool calls.
scenes.narration: The receipt is inspectable. It lists file, line, parser, hash, and the tool calls behind the finding.

scenes.label: Accuracy
scenes.title: The report scores the run against ground truth.
scenes.narration: The accuracy report compares the run to ground truth. It records sixteen confirmed claims, one revoked claim, zero hallucinations, and precision and recall of one point zero.

scenes.label: Tests
scenes.title: The guardrails are tested.
scenes.proof: Path escape, report writing, schema safety, weak claim revocation.
scenes.narration: The tests cover path escape, report writing, schema safety, and weak claim revocation. The demo does not rely on a hidden manual fix.

scenes.label: Boundary
scenes.title: The safety boundary sits below the planner.
scenes.proof: Sealed evidence, typed tools, verifier state, receipts.
scenes.narration: The safety boundary stays below any planner: sealed evidence, typed read-only tools, verifier-owned claim state, and replayable receipts.

scenes.label: Submission
scenes.title: Run it, inspect it, extend it.
scenes.proof: Repo, case, reports, deck, video source, tests.
scenes.narration: The repo includes the case, reports, deck, video source, and tests. Run it, inspect CLAIM zero zero four, then add the next SIFT parser.
